title: penstemon antirrhinoides subsp. microphyllus
intro: Penstemon antirrhinoides subsp. microphyllus, also known as the narrow-leaved beardtongue, is a species of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, known for its narrow, pointed leaves and white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The narrow-leaved beardtongue can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, offering potential health benefits such as relief from digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations.
